summary: The Gemara presents the cosmological tradition: the world was created from Zion outward, with the even ha-shetiya as the primordial foundation point. The Mishna then describes the HP taking the goat blood from the stirrer and re-entering the Holy of Holies for the final inner atonement service. The goat blood atones for all Israel. The section concludes with the livaya concept: the blood services form paired companions that together create complete atonement.

display.whats: The world was created from Zion outward - the foundation stone is the world's navel
display.hint: The Gemara discusses how the world was created from Zion (the Temple Mount) outward. Different opinions describe how the 'even ha-shetiya' is the primordial foundation from which creation radiated.

learning.summary: The Gemara presents the cosmological tradition: the world was not created uniformly but from a single point - Zion/the Temple Mount. The even ha-shetiya is the central anchor point; the rest of the world was created extending outward from it. Various sages present how this creation from the center worked: whether it extended from a single point, from four sides, etc. The concept of 'shetiyah' (the stone's name) means 'foundation' or 'woven from' - the world is woven together from this point.
learning.ahaMoment: Creation radiates outward from Jerusalem's Temple Mount - the entire world is an extension of this sacred foundation.
learning.memoryAnchor: World created from Zion: the even ha-shetiya is where God first created, and the rest followed.
learning.openQuestion: What does it mean theologically that the HP's innermost service occurs at the exact point where creation began?

argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The world was created from Zion outward. The even ha-shetiya is the primordial foundation point of creation.
argumentFlow.label: Teaching: The world was created from Zion outward.

misconceptions: The world was created equally from all points - the Temple Mount is specifically the creation's starting point.

quizSeeds.seed: What does the tradition about the world being created from Zion teach?
quizSeeds.answer: The world was not created uniformly but radiated outward from a single point - the even ha-shetiya at the Temple Mount. The name 'shetiyah' (foundation/woven) reflects this: the world is woven together from this foundation.

sugyot.finalRuling: The world was created from Zion (the even ha-shetiya) outward; creation radiated from the Temple Mount's foundation stone.

display.whats: The HP takes the blood of the goat from the stirrer and enters the Holy of Holies
display.hint: The Mishna describes the HP now taking the goat blood (from the scapegoat's partner that was slaughtered) from whoever has been stirring it. He enters the Holy of Holies with the goat blood for the final inner blood service.

learning.summary: After performing the bull blood service (inner and outer), the HP returns to take the goat blood from the stirrer. He enters the Holy of Holies again with the goat blood and performs the same sprinkling sequence: once upward, seven times downward. The goat blood atones for all Israel (as opposed to the bull blood which atones for the HP and his household). This is the culminating atonement act for the entire Jewish people.
learning.ahaMoment: Bull blood for HP and household; goat blood for all Israel. The HP takes the goat blood and returns inside for the final innermost atonement.
learning.memoryAnchor: HP takes goat blood from stirrer, enters Holy of Holies again - this sprinkling atones for all Israel.
learning.openQuestion: Why does the goat blood service come after the bull blood service in the Yom Kippur blood protocol?

argumentFlow.speaker: Mishna
argumentFlow.text: The HP takes the blood of the goat from the stirrer and enters the Holy of Holies to sprinkle it.
argumentFlow.label: Ruling: The HP takes the blood of the

argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The goat blood atones for all Israel; it follows the bull blood (which atones for the HP and his household).
argumentFlow.label: Explanation: The goat blood atones for all Israel;

argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: Bull blood first (inner, then outer), then goat blood (inner, then outer) - the sequence progresses from most personal to most communal.
argumentFlow.label: Sequence: Bull blood first (inner, then outer), then

halachic: goat blood sprinkled inside Holy of Holies
goat blood atones for all Israel

misconceptions: The goat blood is sprinkled before the bull blood - the bull blood comes first in the mandated sequence.

quizSeeds.seed: What is the goat blood service and when does it occur in the Yom Kippur sequence?
quizSeeds.answer: After the bull blood (inner and outer services), the HP takes the goat blood from the stirrer and enters the Holy of Holies again. He sprinkles once upward and seven times downward. The goat blood atones for all Israel.

sugyot.finalRuling: The HP takes goat blood from the stirrer and re-enters the Holy of Holies; the goat blood atones for all Israel.

display.whats: The livaya (accompanying partner) in Temple service - completion of the blood service sequence
display.hint: The concept of livaya - a person who accompanies or clings to another - is applied to the HP's blood service. This concludes the section and transitions to the next phase of the Yom Kippur service.

learning.summary: The Gemara uses the metaphor of livaya (a clinging partner/companion) to describe the relationship between the blood services: the bull blood and goat blood services accompany each other, completing the full atonement picture. The inner services and outer services form a complete unified atonement. This section concludes the blood service discussion and transitions to the remaining Yom Kippur services.
learning.ahaMoment: The blood services form a unified whole: bull and goat, inner and outer, personal and communal - each pair clings to its partner like a livaya.
learning.memoryAnchor: Livaya: the blood services cling together as paired companions covering all dimensions of atonement.
learning.openQuestion: What is the conceptual unity that makes bull and goat blood, inner and outer services, into a single atonement system?

argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The concept of livaya (clinging companion) describes how the blood services are paired and complete each other.
argumentFlow.label: Concept: The concept of livaya (clinging companion) describes

halachic: blood services form unified atonement system

misconceptions: The blood services are independent - they form an integrated unified atonement system.

visualizableElements.description: Bull blood and goat blood as paired services: inner and outer, personal and communal, each clinging to the other

quizSeeds.seed: What does the concept of livaya teach about the Yom Kippur blood services?
quizSeeds.answer: The blood services form paired companions (livaya): bull and goat, inner and outer, personal and communal. Together they constitute a unified atonement system covering all categories of sin and all of Israel.

sugyot.finalRuling: The blood services are paired (livaya) - bull/goat and inner/outer forming a unified atonement covering all Israel.

rashiTranslations.en: Like a man clinging to his companion between his arms - the world clings together with the even ha-shetiya.
He spread them to the market - he unfolded the curtains from the wall.
Created from Zion - Zion was created first and all other lands were attached to it from all sides.
A solid mass - it had one solid mass and other clumps attached to its sides to make the world.
Created from the sides - it had four solid masses at the corners and they expanded and joined in the middle.
For snow He says 'be earth' - the earth was made: from where does rain come? From four sides He sent it down and it formed the earth.
'From Zion, the perfection of beauty' - the following verse continues this...
The generations of the heavens - the luminaries and stars.
The text reads: the generations of the heavens were created from the heavens, and the generations of the earth from the earth.
As one who clings to a companion between his arms - the blood services cling together.
The bull blood and goat blood services are paired companions in the Yom Kippur atonement.
The inner services and outer services complete each other.
This concludes the blood service section; the remaining Yom Kippur service begins.
